mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an approaching economic contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical trend level reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO remains above the threshold for consecutive months, indicating that capacity utilization peaks precede equity drawdowns rather than rallies.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, directly translating into positive forward equity returns over a one-month horizon as corporate profits scale with economic activity.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 20-day forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero across multiple decades, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the Federal Reserve's implicit target forces monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly window.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite inflation exceeding the threshold, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates a negative information coefficient during periods of labor force participation decline, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging past cyclical peaks signals peak consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically precedes short-term equity strength as related supply chains and financial sectors benefit.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when housing starts exceed the threshold while real interest rates remain positive, indicating that credit conditions override housing momentum as a market driver.
